handles: creating → delegate ULID minting to HasPrefixedUlid trait (prefix `gfc_`)
creating → default metadata={} when null (jsonb column has default but PHP-side we ensure it never lands as null)
creating → refuse when subject_type is not a registered #[GeofenceSubjectAlias] (unless geofencing.morph_map.strict = false)
creating → refuse when fenceable_type is not a registered #[Geofenceable] (unless geofencing.morph_map.strict = false)
creating → refuse when supersedes_check_id references a row in a different tenant (cross-tenant defence)
creating → refuse when supersedes_check_id references a row with different fenceable_type/fenceable_id (audit chain integrity)
created → fire GeofenceCheckCreated (domain event; distinct from GeofenceEvaluated which is fired by the SERVICE)
updating → refuse via RuntimeException from the model's saving hook (immutability guard). Observer never receives an update event.
deleting → accept only when the actor is a platform_admin (retention path) OR the caller is the SoftDeletes internal flow
observers.immutability_note: The immutability guard is on the MODEL's booted() `saving` hook — not this observer. The observer's `updating` never fires because the exception aborts before it.

trait_boot_hooks.target: any consumer model using the trait
hooks: updated → if any of the 7 fence columns changed, fire FenceableGeometryUpdated event with { fenceable_type, fenceable_id, changed_fields[], updated_by }

saving → if changing geofence_polygon / location_point / geofence_radius_m / geofence_accuracy_tolerance_m / geofence_enforcement_enabled, set geofence_updated_at = now() + geofence_updated_by = auth()->id()
trait_boot_hooks.note: These hooks run on the CONSUMER's model — not on GeofenceCheck. Enables the module to invalidate its cache when downstream models edit their fences without any downstream code.
